---
title: "Data analysis_P3"
author: "Mahfuza Haque Mahi"
date: "2026-04-16"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
library(tidyverse)
library(pwr)
library(knitr)
library(kableExtra)
library(powertools)
library(ggplot2)
library(reshape2)
library(tidyverse)
library(skimr)
library(tableone)
library(gridExtra)
library(here)
library(table1)
library(ggplot2)
library(gridExtra)
library(corrplot)
library(survival)
library(survminer)
library(glmnet)
library(car)
df <- read.csv(here::here("Project3/DataRaw", "frmgham2.csv"))
```


```{r}
# -------------------------
# Create baseline dataset
# -------------------------
# Period 1 only, and exclude prior stroke at baseline
df_base <- df %>%
  filter(PERIOD == 1, PREVSTRK == 0)

# -------------------------
# Create variables for Table 1
# -------------------------
# Keep missing values in the dataset
# Recode variables for readability
# Also create a 10-year incident stroke indicator for descriptive Table 1

df_table1 <- df_base %>%
  mutate(
    sex = factor(SEX, levels = c(1, 2), labels = c("Male", "Female")),

    curr_smoke = factor(CURSMOKE,
                        levels = c(0, 1),
                        labels = c("No", "Yes")),

    diabetes = factor(DIABETES,
                      levels = c(0, 1),
                      labels = c("No", "Yes")),

    bp_meds = factor(BPMEDS,
                     levels = c(0, 1),
                     labels = c("No", "Yes")),

    prev_chd = factor(PREVCHD,
                      levels = c(0, 1),
                      labels = c("No", "Yes")),

    # 10-year incident stroke indicator for baseline cohort
    # event if stroke occurred within 10 years
    stroke_10yr = case_when(
      !is.na(TIMESTRK) & TIMESTRK <= 3652.5 ~ "Yes",
      !is.na(TIMESTRK) & TIMESTRK > 3652.5  ~ "No",
      TRUE ~ NA_character_
    ),
    stroke_10yr = factor(stroke_10yr, levels = c("No", "Yes"))
  ) %>%
  select(
    sex,
    AGE,
    SYSBP,
    BMI,
    TOTCHOL,
    prev_chd,
    diabetes,
    curr_smoke,
    bp_meds,
    stroke_10yr
  )

# -------------------------
# Label variables
# -------------------------
label(df_table1$AGE)        <- "Age (years)"
label(df_table1$SYSBP)      <- "Systolic BP (mmHg)"
label(df_table1$BMI)        <- "BMI (kg/m^2)"
label(df_table1$TOTCHOL)    <- "Total Cholesterol (mg/dL)"
label(df_table1$prev_chd)   <- "Coronary Heart Disease"
label(df_table1$diabetes)   <- "Diabetes"
label(df_table1$curr_smoke) <- "Current Smoker"
label(df_table1$bp_meds)    <- "Antihypertensive Medication"
label(df_table1$stroke_10yr)<- "Incident Stroke within 10 Years"

# -------------------------
# Create Table 1
# -------------------------
tab1 <- table1(
  ~ AGE + SYSBP + BMI + TOTCHOL + prev_chd + diabetes + curr_smoke + bp_meds + stroke_10yr | sex,
  data = df_table1,
  overall = "Overall",
  caption = "Baseline Characteristics of study participants by Sex"
)

tab1
```

```{r}
# -----------------------------
# Create BP group
# -----------------------------
df_surv <- df_surv %>%
  mutate(
    bp_group = ifelse(SYSBP >= 160, "High BP", "Normal BP"),
    bp_group = factor(bp_group, levels = c("Normal BP", "High BP"))
  )

# -----------------------------
# KM for Diabetes
# -----------------------------
fit_dm <- survfit(Surv(time, event) ~ DIABETES, data = df_surv)

p1 <- ggsurvplot(
  fit_dm,
  data = df_surv,
  conf.int = TRUE,
  pval = TRUE,
  risk.table = FALSE,
  legend.labs = c("No Diabetes", "Diabetes"),
  title = "A. Diabetes Status",
  xlab = "Time (days)",
  ylab = "Stroke-free survival probability",
  ggtheme = theme_minimal()
)

# -----------------------------
# KM for BP group
# -----------------------------
fit_bp <- survfit(Surv(time, event) ~ bp_group, data = df_surv)

p2 <- ggsurvplot(
  fit_bp,
  data = df_surv,
  conf.int = TRUE,
  pval = TRUE,
  risk.table = FALSE,
  legend.labs = c("Normal BP", "High BP"),
  title = "B. Blood Pressure Group",
  xlab = "Time (days)",
  ylab = "Stroke-free survival probability",
  ggtheme = theme_minimal()
)

# -----------------------------
# Combine side-by-side
# -----------------------------
#grid.arrange(p1$plot, p2$plot, ncol = 2)
grid.arrange(
  p1$plot, p2$plot,
  ncol = 2,
  bottom = "Kaplan–Meier curves for 10-year stroke-free survival by diabetes and blood pressure group."
)
```



```{r}
# =========================
# Create survival dataset
# =========================

df_surv <- df_base %>%
  mutate(
    # time to event (stroke) or censoring at 10 years
    time = pmin(TIMESTRK, 3652.5, na.rm = TRUE),

    # event indicator: 1 = stroke within 10 years, 0 = censored
    event = ifelse(!is.na(TIMESTRK) & TIMESTRK <= 3652.5, 1, 0)
  )

# Check
#summary(df_surv$time)
#table(df_surv$event)
```

```{r}
# =========================
# LASSO variable selection for Cox model
# Force AGE, SYSBP, DIABETES into the model
# Select among: PREVCHD, BPMEDS, CURSMOKE, TOTCHOL, BMI
# =========================

#-----------------------------------
# Keep only variables needed here
#-----------------------------------
lasso_vars <- c("SEX", "AGE", "SYSBP", "DIABETES",
                "PREVCHD", "BPMEDS", "CURSMOKE", "TOTCHOL", "BMI",
                "time", "event")

df_lasso <- df_surv %>%
  select(all_of(lasso_vars))

#-----------------------------------
# Function to run sex-specific LASSO
#-----------------------------------
run_lasso_cox <- function(data, sex_value) {
  
  # subset by sex
  dat_sex <- data %>%
    filter(SEX == sex_value) %>%
    
    # complete case only for variables used in this LASSO step
    select(AGE, SYSBP, DIABETES, PREVCHD, BPMEDS, CURSMOKE, TOTCHOL, BMI, time, event) %>%
    na.omit()
  
  # design matrix
  x <- model.matrix(
    ~ AGE + SYSBP + DIABETES + PREVCHD + BPMEDS + CURSMOKE + TOTCHOL + BMI,
    data = dat_sex
  )[, -1]   # remove intercept
  
  # survival outcome
  y <- Surv(dat_sex$time, dat_sex$event)
  
  # penalty factors:
  # 0 = forced in model
  # 1 = subject to LASSO selection
  penalty_factors <- c(
    AGE = 0,
    SYSBP = 0,
    DIABETES = 0,
    PREVCHD = 1,
    BPMEDS = 1,
    CURSMOKE = 1,
    TOTCHOL = 1,
    BMI = 1
  )
  
  # make sure order matches columns of x
  penalty_factors <- penalty_factors[colnames(x)]
  
  # cross-validated LASSO Cox model
  cvfit <- cv.glmnet(
    x = x,
    y = y,
    family = "cox",
    alpha = 1,
    penalty.factor = penalty_factors,
    nfolds = 10
  )
  
  # coefficients at lambda.min
  coef_min <- coef(cvfit, s = "lambda.min")
  coef_1se <- coef(cvfit, s = "lambda.1se")
  
  selected_min <- rownames(coef_min)[as.vector(coef_min != 0)]
  selected_1se <- rownames(coef_1se)[as.vector(coef_1se != 0)]
  
  list(
    data_used = dat_sex,
    cvfit = cvfit,
    selected_lambda_min = selected_min,
    selected_lambda_1se = selected_1se,
    coef_lambda_min = coef_min,
    coef_lambda_1se = coef_1se
  )
}

#-----------------------------------
# Run separately for males and females
#-----------------------------------
lasso_male   <- run_lasso_cox(df_lasso, sex_value = 1)
lasso_female <- run_lasso_cox(df_lasso, sex_value = 2)

#-----------------------------------
# View selected variables
#-----------------------------------
lasso_male$selected_lambda_min
lasso_female$selected_lambda_min

lasso_male$selected_lambda_1se
lasso_female$selected_lambda_1se
```
```{r}
# =========================
# Final Cox models
# =========================

# Final variables
final_vars <- c("AGE", "SYSBP", "DIABETES", "PREVCHD", 
                "BPMEDS", "CURSMOKE", "BMI", "TOTCHOL", 
                "time", "event", "SEX")

df_final <- df_surv %>%
  select(all_of(final_vars)) %>%
  na.omit()   # complete case ONLY for final model variables

# -------------------------
# Male model
# -------------------------
cox_male <- coxph(
  Surv(time, event) ~ AGE + SYSBP + DIABETES + PREVCHD + 
    BPMEDS + CURSMOKE + BMI + TOTCHOL,
  data = df_final %>% filter(SEX == 1)
)

#summary(cox_male)

# -------------------------
# Female model
# -------------------------
cox_female <- coxph(
  Surv(time, event) ~ AGE + SYSBP + DIABETES + PREVCHD + 
    BPMEDS + CURSMOKE + BMI + TOTCHOL,
  data = df_final %>% filter(SEX == 2)
)

#summary(cox_female)

# Create a variable label mapping for a nice summary table
var_labels <- c(
  AGE = "Age (per year)",
  SYSBP = "Systolic Blood Pressure (mmHg)",
  DIABETES = "Diabetes (Yes vs No)",
  PREVCHD = "Coronary Heart Disease",
  BPMEDS = "Antihypertensive Medication",
  CURSMOKE = "Current Smoker",
  BMI = "Body Mass Index (kg/m²)",
  TOTCHOL = "Total Cholesterol (mg/dL)"
)

# Function to tidy Cox output
tidy_cox <- function(model, sex_label) {
  broom::tidy(model, exponentiate = TRUE, conf.int = TRUE) %>%
    mutate(
      Sex = sex_label,
      HR = estimate,
      CI = paste0("(", round(conf.low, 2), ", ", round(conf.high, 2), ")")
    ) %>%
    select(Sex, term, HR, CI, p.value)
}

# Combine male + female
cox_table <- bind_rows(
  tidy_cox(cox_male, "Male"),
  tidy_cox(cox_female, "Female")
)

cox_table_clean <- cox_table %>%
  mutate(
    Variable = var_labels[term],   # replace names
    HR = round(HR, 2),
    p.value = round(p.value, 3)
  ) %>%
  select(Sex, Variable, HR, CI, p.value)  # reorder & rename

# Display
cox_table_clean %>%
  kable(caption = "Hazard ratios from sex-specific Cox proportional hazards models") %>%
  kable_styling(full_width = FALSE)

# Extract VIF values
vif_male <- data.frame(
  Variable = names(vif(cox_male)),
  Male = as.numeric(vif(cox_male))
)

vif_female <- data.frame(
  Variable = names(vif(cox_female)),
  Female = as.numeric(vif(cox_female))
)

# Merge
vif_table <- left_join(vif_male, vif_female, by = "Variable")

# Apply nice labels
var_labels <- c(
  AGE = "Age",
  SYSBP = "Systolic Blood Pressure",
  DIABETES = "Diabetes",
  PREVCHD = "Coronary Heart Disease",
  BPMEDS = "Antihypertensive Medication",
  CURSMOKE = "Current Smoker",
  BMI = "Body Mass Index",
  TOTCHOL = "Total Cholesterol"
)

vif_table <- vif_table %>%
  mutate(
    Variable = var_labels[Variable],
    Male = round(Male, 2),
    Female = round(Female, 2)
  )

# Print nice table
vif_table %>%
  kable(caption = "Variance Inflation Factors (VIF) for covariates in male and female Cox models") %>%
  kable_styling(full_width = FALSE)
```

```{r}
# --------------------------------------------------------
# Forest plot of hazard ratios with confidence intervals
# --------------------------------------------------------
cox_plot <- cox_table_clean %>%
  mutate(
    lower = as.numeric(sub("\\((.*),.*", "\\1", CI)),
    upper = as.numeric(sub(".*, (.*)\\)", "\\1", CI))
  )

ggplot(cox_plot, aes(x = Variable, y = HR, ymin = lower, ymax = upper, color = Sex)) +
  geom_pointrange(position = position_dodge(width = 0.5)) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 1, linetype = "dashed") +
  coord_flip() +
  labs(title = "Hazard Ratios from Cox Models",
       y = "Hazard Ratio",
       x = "") +
  theme_minimal()
```


```{r}
# -----------------------------------------------------------------
# Check proportional hazards assumption using Schoenfeld residuals
# -----------------------------------------------------------------

# Male
ph_male <- cox.zph(cox_male)
par(mfrow = c(3,3), oma = c(0, 0, 3, 0))
plot(ph_male)
mtext("Schoenfeld residual plots for the male Cox model",
      side = 3, outer = TRUE, cex = 1.1)
par(mfrow = c(1,1))

# Female
ph_female <- cox.zph(cox_female)
par(mfrow = c(3,3), oma = c(0, 0, 3, 0))
plot(ph_female)
mtext("Schoenfeld residual plots for the female Cox model",
      side = 3, outer = TRUE, cex = 1.1)
par(mfrow = c(1,1))
```

```{r}
# -----------------------------
# Helper: get baseline values
# -----------------------------
get_baseline_profile <- function(data) {
  data %>%
    summarise(
      SYSBP = mean(SYSBP, na.rm = TRUE),
      BMI = mean(BMI, na.rm = TRUE),
      TOTCHOL = mean(TOTCHOL, na.rm = TRUE),
      DIABETES = 0,
      PREVCHD = 0,
      BPMEDS = 0,
      CURSMOKE = 0
    )
}

# -----------------------------
# Function to compute survival
# -----------------------------
compute_surv_table <- function(cox_model, data, sex_value) {
  
  df_sex <- data %>% filter(SEX == sex_value)
  
  base <- get_baseline_profile(df_sex)
  
  ages <- c(40, 50, 60)
  
  profiles <- list(
    baseline = base,
    
    high_bp = base %>% mutate(SYSBP = 160),
    
    diabetes = base %>% mutate(DIABETES = 1),
    
    high_bp_diabetes = base %>% mutate(SYSBP = 160, DIABETES = 1),
    
    smoker = base %>% mutate(CURSMOKE = 1)
  )
  
  results <- lapply(ages, function(a) {
    
    lapply(names(profiles), function(p) {
      
      newdata <- profiles[[p]] %>%
        mutate(AGE = a)
      
      sf <- survfit(cox_model, newdata = newdata)
      
      # Extract survival at 10 years
      surv_10yr <- summary(sf, times = 3652.5)$surv
      
      data.frame(
        Age = a,
        Profile = p,
        Survival_10yr = surv_10yr
      )
    }) %>% bind_rows()
    
  }) %>% bind_rows()
  
  # reshape to wide
  results %>%
    pivot_wider(names_from = Profile, values_from = Survival_10yr) %>%
    arrange(Age)
}

# -----------------------------
# Male table
# -----------------------------
male_table <- compute_surv_table(cox_male, df_final, sex_value = 1)

# -----------------------------
# Female table
# -----------------------------
female_table <- compute_surv_table(cox_female, df_final, sex_value = 2)
```

```{r}
# Male table
male_table %>%
  kable(digits = 3, caption = "10-year stroke-free survival probabilities for males") %>%
  kable_styling(full_width = FALSE)

# Female table
female_table %>%
  kable(digits = 3, caption = "10-year stroke-free survival probabilities for females") %>%
  kable_styling(full_width = FALSE)
```
